common.header: V INTERNATIONAL FARABI FORUM: THE 1156th ANNIVERSARY OF AL-FARABI

farabi-article.title: Commemorating the 1156th Anniversary of the Great Thinker
paragraphs: Al-Farabi (c. 870), also known as Abu Nasr Al-Farabi, was one of the greatest philosophers and scientists of the medieval Islamic world. He was born in the region of Farab (in present-day Kazakhstan) and became known as the “Second Teacher” after Aristotle due to his deep contributions to philosophy and logic.

Al-Farabi made significant advances in fields such as philosophy, mathematics, music theory, and political science. His works focused on the relationship between reason and knowledge, the structure of an ideal society, and the role of education. He had a lasting influence on both Eastern and Western thinkers, including scholars of the Islamic Golden Age and later European philosophers.

The year 2026 marks the 1156th anniversary of the birth of the great scientist, philosopher, mathematician, and musician Al-Farabi. His works have had a profound influence on the development of science, education, and culture, not only in the East but across the entire world.
In honor of this significant date, an international forum is being held to discuss Al-Farabi’s works and his contributions to the development of philosophy, ethics, pedagogy, and modern science.
farabi-article.quote: «Science makes a person noble and worthy of respect»

sections.name: International Scientific and Practical Conference: «The Heritage of Al-Farabi and Global Civilization: the unity of Philosophy, Science, and Spirituality»

timeline.description: Zhakypbek Altayev — Scientific Advisor of the Al-Farabi Center, Al-Farabi KazNU, Doctor of Philosophical Sciences, Professor. Topic: “The History of Farabi Studies in Kazakhstan”

Ashirbek Muminov — Advisor on International Relations at IRCICA, Doctor of Historical Sciences, Professor. Topic: “Manuscripts of Abu Nasr Al-Farabi’s Works in the Collections of the Republic of Turkey”

Natalya Seitakhmetova — Chief Researcher at IPPSRS, Academician of the NAS RK, Doctor of Philosophical Sciences, Professor. Topic: “The Discourse of Intellect in Al-Farabi’s Teachings: Continuity of Ideas in the Era of Digitalization and AI”

Mustafa Jalel Altynshash — Vice Dean of the Faculty of Theology at Istanbul University, PhD, Associate Professor. Topic: “Manuscripts of Abu Nasr al-Farabi in the collections of the Süleymaniye Library”

Askarbek Bekboev — Institute of Philosophy named after A.A. Altmyshbaev, Academician, Doctor of Philosophical Sciences. Topic: “Al-Farabi, Aitmatov, and AI: From the Philosophy of Mind to Algorithmic Rationality”
Ali Rıza Gül — Doctor of Philosophical Sciences, Professor (Eskishehir, Turkey). Topic: “The Unity of Knowledge and Virtue in Al-Farabi: A Conceptual Analysis”
